rulings.Case Title: SUSY CHEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Petition For Writ
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 23, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER SUSY CHEN Amended Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Petition For Writ is DENIED. Request for leave to amend is two-fold: (1) in the moving papers Petitioner seeks to add a new cause of action for CEQA; arguments in opposition are well-taken and Petitioner fails to address these arguments or support this request in the reply. Therefore, leave to amend to asserts a new cause of action is DENIED. (2) Petitioner also seeks to include new factual allegations pertaining to the existing claim. This action was filed on August 8, 2022. The facts giving rise to the new factual allegations arose in October 2022. This request is not properly brought on a motion for leave to amend. Petitioner may file a motion to file a supplemental complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAMAR D BURKS ET AL VS. TERRENCE LEROY SANDLES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 23, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF LAMAR BURKS, JOHN SANDLES, CLEO BRAZIER, ANDRE SANDLES, SANDRENA SANDLES Motion For Possession Of Real Property is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that removal of the co-owner from the property may be accomplished by a motion filed by Plaintiffs. Interlocutory judgment vests the power to "evict or facilitate the eviction of owner-occupants and/or tenants" and the referee shall proceed accordingly. (Interlocutory Judgment, paragraph 13(c)). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
